title: Allowing public ACLs or policies on a S3 bucket is security-sensitive
description: Allowing public ACLs or policies on a S3 bucket is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotawscweBy default S3 buckets are private, it means that only the bucket owner can access it.This access control can be relaxed with ACLs or policies.To prevent permissive policies to be set on a S3 bucket the following settings can be configured:BlockPublicAcls: to block or not public ACLs to be set to the S3 bucket.IgnorePublicAcls: to consider or not existing public ACLs set to the S3 bucket.BlockPublicPolicy: to block or not public policies to be set to the S3 bucket.RestrictPublicBuckets: to restrict or not the access to the S3 endpoints of public policies to the principals within the bucket owner account.Ask Yourself WhetherThe S3 bucket stores sensitive
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: my-public-bucket
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: false  # Non-compliant
        BlockPublicPolicy: false  # Non-compliant
        IgnorePublicAcls: false
        RestrictPublicBuckets: false
compliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: my-secure-bucket
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true  # Compliant
        BlockPublicPolicy: true  # Compliant
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

check_properties.message: S3 bucket allows public ACLs (BlockPublicAcls should be true)
S3 bucket allows public policies (BlockPublicPolicy should be true)
S3 bucket considers public ACLs (IgnorePublicAcls should be true)
S3 bucket allows unrestricted public access (RestrictPublicBuckets should be true)
